preview_text:
England enter as the model's clear favorite, assigned a 63% win probability against Panama. The underdog is given a 12% chance to prevail, with a draw accounting for the remaining 25% of the distribution.

The spread reflects a substantial gap in expected strength. England's win probability sits roughly 50 percentage points above Panama's, suggesting the model expects England to control much of the match. A draw remains a material outcome at one-quarter probability, indicating the match carries enough competitive uncertainty to make a goalless result or equal scoring plausible even if England's superiority is pronounced.

The sportsbook consensus materially favors England beyond the model's assessment. Books price England's win at 76%, compared to the model's 63% — a gap of 13 percentage points. The market also assigns Panama only 9% to win versus the model's 12%, and shows little appetite for a draw at 15%. Polymarket prices align directionally with sportsbooks but push England's odds even higher. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is more accurate; the divergence merits attention from users seeking multiple forecasting perspectives.
